title: Implement guest-mode UI labeling as a grid/sidebar checklist with clear locked/CTA messaging

requirements.text:
Add a guest-mode access checklist component that presents the user-facing labels for what is available vs locked in Guest Mode in a layout-ready format (responsive grid on desktop and sidebar/stacked layout on small screens), using the exact labels:
- Visible to guests (Read-only/Limited):
  - “Study (Read-only)”
  - “Videos (Read-only)”
  - “Parts & Specs (Read-only)”
  - “Suppliers (Read-only)”
  - “AI Assistant (Limited)”
- Locked for guests (Sign in to unlock):
  - “Jobs (Sign in to unlock)”
  - “Logs (Sign in to unlock)”
  - “Full Diagnostics (Sign in to unlock)”
  - “Study Progress (Sign in to unlock)”
  - “Community (Sign in to unlock)”
  - “Export Data (Sign in to unlock)”
acceptanceCriteria: When the app is in Guest Mode (as determined by existing isGuest logic), the dashboard shows a clearly readable Guest Mode checklist with the exact labels specified.
The checklist renders as a multi-column grid on wider screens and a sidebar/stacked layout on small screens without overflow or layout breakage.
No user-facing label text in the checklist is missing, renamed, or paraphrased.

requirements.text: Provide a clear upgrade call-to-action from the guest-mode checklist: include a “Create an account”/“Sign in to unlock” action that opens the existing ProfileSetupModal in guest-upgrade mode (isGuestUpgrade=true) without navigating away from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: From the guest-mode checklist, clicking the CTA opens ProfileSetupModal with upgrade copy (isGuestUpgrade=true).
Closing/dismissing the modal returns the user to the same dashboard state without a full page reload.

requirements.text:
Align guest-mode restrictions with the checklist by ensuring guest users cannot persist or export data where the checklist says features are locked, and show a consistent, user-facing message when a blocked action is attempted:
- Jobs: blocked create/save (already gated)
- Logs: blocked add/save; exporting data must be blocked (and show a message)
- Full Diagnostics: block saving diagnostic sessions / exporting diagnostic reports when isGuest=true
- Community posting: block any posting/saving actions if present, or clearly indicate it is locked when isGuest=true
- Study progress tracking: ensure no guest progress persistence calls are made (if any exist)

quotes: What should we do with the guest mode

acceptanceCriteria: In Guest Mode, attempting any blocked action results in a clear error/toast message telling the user to create an account to save/unlock the feature.
DataLogging export is blocked for guests even when there are entries available, and the user is informed it requires a full account.
Diagnostic session persistence/export actions are blocked in Guest Mode, and the user is informed it requires a full account.
Non-blocked read-only areas remain accessible in Guest Mode (Study, Videos, Parts & Specs, Suppliers, and limited AI assistant usage where no saving occurs).

constraints: Frontend immutable paths must not be edited: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui/*.
Use English for any user-facing text.
Do not add third-party auth; only Internet Identity.
Do not add external databases or backend services; keep within the existing Internet Computer + Motoko single-actor backend architecture.
nonGoals: Do not add new Study modules or modify existing study-module educational content in this change request.
Do not implement real-time chat, push notifications, or external integrations.
Do not redesign the entire dashboard navigation; only add the guest-mode labeling/checklist and ensure guest restrictions match it.
